label: PASS
rationale: Locking_Range1 is preconfigured with RangeStart 0, RangeLength 0, false lock-enable/lock-state booleans, and LockOnReset Power Cycle [0]. The Admin Get ACL covers RangeStart through ActiveKey, so a successful Get of columns 3 through 9 returning those stored values is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: There is no prior successful Locking Set changing RangeLength, and the Opal Locking_Range1 preconfiguration sets RangeLength to 0. Returning column 4 as 8 in the composite Get does not match the stored row state.

label: PASS
rationale: The second successful Locking Set writes columns 3 through 9 to 120, 12, false, true, true, false, and [0,3]. Core requires all Set changes to be made on success, and the authorized composite Get returns those readable cells.

label: FAIL
rationale: The final successful Locking Set wrote LockOnReset to [0,3] along with the other returned columns. A later authorized Get returning column 9 as only [0] fails to reflect the successful Set value.

label: PASS
rationale: After the full-row Set, the later successful Set changes only WriteLockEnabled to false and WriteLocked to true; unmentioned cells retain their prior values. The target Get of columns 5 through 9 returns true, false, true, true, and [0,3], matching the current stored row state.

label: FAIL
rationale: The later successful Set explicitly changes WriteLocked to true while changing WriteLockEnabled to false. Even though WriteLocked is ignored for locking behavior when WriteLockEnabled is false, it remains the stored column value and should be returned as true by the authorized Get.

label: PASS
rationale: A successful Set writes columns 3 through 9 to 64, 16, true, true, false, false, and an empty LockOnReset set. Core defines an empty LockOnReset set as no lock-state change on reset, and the authorized composite Get returns the same stored values.

label: FAIL
rationale: The prior successful Locking Set wrote LockOnReset to the empty set. Since successful Set applies all changed cells and Core gives meaning to an empty LockOnReset set, the later Get should return [], not the preconfigured [0].
